title: The Big Cheese Quick Click Mouse Trap - Twinpack, Kills Mice, Baited and Ready-To-Use, Easy Clean and Disposal, Plastic, Indoor and Outdoor Use, White
features: ALL-IN-ONE KIT: Pre-baited with Fresh ‘n Tasty bait attractant which is proven to catch more mice, ready-to-use
POWERFUL MECHANISM: Double the snap and trap power, Improved mechanism easier to set than traditional snap traps for mice and still provide a quick and effective kill
EASY-TO-SET: Mouse traps simply need one touch to set, Caught rodents can easily be disposed of by squeezing the trap back open
REUSABLE: Plastic design makes the mouse trap easy to clean, offering a more hygienic alternative to traditional wooden snap traps
RE-BAIT: Use The Big Cheese Mouse and Rat Attractant (sold separately)
description: From the manufacturer OVER 25 YEARS OF RODENT CONTROL MADE EASY! Catch & Kill: We offer a wide selection of conventional spring traps with a powerful snap and hold. Our Fresh 'n Tasty range is ready-baited with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant - a poison-free formula proven to dramatically increase catch rate. Pet-safe and easy-set options are also available making The Big Cheese Catch & Kill range suitable for use in most areas of the household. Bait & Kill: Our Home Choice range offers a bait for every situation, and with 50% LESS POISON than previous formulations, it is the ideal choice for use in and around the home. The range includes damp proof wax blocks suitable for use in all weather conditions, chew thru grain bait sachets for easy bait placement, and highly palatable fresh pasta bait recommended for food preparation areas. Follow our easy on-pack icons to choose the most effective bait for your situation. Catch Alive: Safe for use around children and pets, these traps provide a humane, welfare-friendly alternative to lethal methods. Our professional-quality cage traps and ready-baited mouse traps have been designed with safety in mind, both for the user and the target pest. Easy-set mechanisms and fast-action closures allow for efficient rodent trapping. Repel: 24/7 protection and peace of mind - our advanced ultrasonic repellers will protect your home around the clock, ridding your home of rodent pests and preventing their return. Plus a new selection of low-cost consumables designed to help defend homes from rodent infestations. Poison-free and safe for use around children. Ready-to-use, one click to set mouse trap Rodent control made easy! When mice and rats move in, it's time to bring in The Big Cheese! Choose from a comprehensive range of tried and tested traps, deterrents and baits within 4 straight-forward methods of control. Quick Click Mouse Trap - 3Pk from The Big Cheese Fresh baited, tested and proven to catch more rodents. Triple pack of new improved traps. Quick Click Mouse Traps are supplied baited, ready-to-use. Double the snap and holding power. The one-quick-click design makes them easier to set than traditional traps and also allows quick and easy disposal of captured mice. Use multiple traps in severely affected areas: mice live in groups and traps placed 2-3 metres apart will ensure the maximum catch. Powerful double spring mechanism guarantees a quick kill. One click to set and another click to dispose of catch. Fresh baited and ready to use. Suitable for indoor and outdoor use. Refill with Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant (purchased separately). Part of the comprehensive range of rodent control products from The Big Cheese. Rat variant also available (product code STV150). Product Highlights Poison free Fresh 'n Tasty bait Poison free and proven to attract rodents with an incredible catch rate compared to scented pedals and other traditional attractants. Reusable Easy to reuse and re-bait, simply clean the trap and re-bait using The Big Cheese Fresh n' Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant. Re-set your trap in one simple, quick click. Powerful Powerful double spring mechanism for a sure kill. The corrosion resistant spring maintains strength and performance use after use ensuring an instant humane kill. Indoor and outdoor use Suitable for indoor and outdoor use. Rodent Problems No-one likes the thought of a mouse or rat in their home so spotting the signs early can help avoid larger infestations. Look out for: droppings, gnaw marks, scratches, dead or live rodents, smells, noises and unusual pet behaviour. Choose the control method from The Big Cheese that's right for you. For immediate problems and prevent return, by seal gaps in walls, remove food sources and use repellent products from The Big Cheese. For more information on rodent control and identifying and preventing rodent problems in your home visit the STV Pest Control website. Diet As one can imagine, the palate of a house mouse is highly adaptable since every habitat they migrate through will hold different opportunities for feeding. Outside dwellers will eat insects and a lot of grain, however, inside dwelling mice are practically omnivorous and, just like brown rats, will consume 10-15% of their body weight each day in just about anything we throw away. Most house mice will nibble on multiple different food sources to obtain their required nutrition, up to 20 times each night, since they’re nocturnal. A large proportion of a mouse’s 3g daily intake will be grain, namely cereal, favouring high-energy, man-made foods. Studies have proven that the levels of aggression in both rats and mice can be attributed to this high fat ‘human’ diet they have both been on for so long. These foods increase oestrogen levels and results in a shorter temper and longer periods of aggression. Evidence of intrusion 3-6 mm long droppings, black and rod-shaped. Approximately 80 per day, often appearing in clusters. Spotted in various places including attics, storage boxes, floorboards, disused cupboards. Check around fridges, ovens, heating units and washing machines too. Check wood, paper, cables, carpet and furniture for marks. Dark grey marks on surfaces from oil on hair. Although they don’t sweat, they emit a musky odour and urinate a lot, producing an ammonia smell. Hearing pattering feet in walls at night could be a sign of a mouse or rat infestation. If your dog is barking at night at what seems to be nothing, it could be picking up the scent or sounds of rodents.
